mission.title: cilium: Differentiate between UDP and TCP services
mission.description: Differentiate between UDP and TCP services. Requested by 30+ users.

steps.title: Check current cilium deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cilium version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
helm list -n cilium 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cilium installation.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
Currently, we do not differentiate between UDP and TCP services both in the BPF datapath and cilium-agent which means that services of different L4 protocols with the same port number cannot co-exist.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Differentiate between UDP and TCP services
steps.description:
Saving protocol information in the lb(x)_key in bpf, as well as
adding protocol information to service maps creation in the
lbmap package ensures that translation, receiving, and forwarding
will always take the protocol into proper account.
```yaml
Cilium will show it as a single service:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Differentiate between UDP and TCP services" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Saving protocol information in the lb(x)_key in bpf, as well as
adding protocol information to service maps creation in the
lbmap package ensures that translation, receiving, and forwarding
will always take the protocol into proper account.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
